tasks_completed.description: Add */docs/research/ write-path prefix to ba role in tool-policy.v1.json (M1)
tasks_completed.changes: Added */docs/research/ to ba.allowed_write_path_prefixes; confirmed present at line 139
tasks_completed.rationale: PRIMARY blocker: pretool-tool-policy.py fires for all tools (matcher *) and checks is_allowed('ba','Write','/root/orchestra/docs/research/case-3') which returned (False,'not in allowed_write_path_prefixes'). Adding */docs/research/ unblocks ba subagent redirect targets in that directory.

tasks_completed.description: Extend sentinel block to IS_SUBAGENT=1 in pretool-bash-safety.sh (M2)
tasks_completed.changes: Removed IS_SUBAGENT != 1 guard at former line 483; IS_SUBAGENT guard at line 145 (legacy allowlist firewall) preserved unchanged
tasks_completed.rationale: SECONDARY blocker: sentinel check was entirely skipped for IS_SUBAGENT=1. Only user-created structured sentinel grants (written by userprompt-consent-allowlist.sh) are extended to subagent contexts.

tasks_completed.description: Align task ID reader fallback to session_id in pretool-bash-safety.sh (M3)
tasks_completed.changes: Reader now falls back to d.get('session_id','') directly (same source as writer's SID) rather than preferring d.get('task_id','') first
tasks_completed.rationale: Mismatch scenario: CLAUDE_TASK_ID unset, PreToolUse JSON task_id field differs from session_id. Writer uses CLAUDE_TASK_ID:-SID (session_id). Reader now uses same session_id as final fallback.

tasks_completed.description: Skip sentinel write for is_regex=true grants in userprompt-consent-allowlist.sh (S1)
tasks_completed.changes: Added early exit (exit 0) at Step 4 when IS_REGEX=true; regex grants still write legacy pattern-string grant only
tasks_completed.rationale: CF-1 fix: regex sentinel writes op:'*' which never structurally matches, causing CF-1 to suppress the legacy /allow path. Skipping sentinel write for regex grants preserves legacy path.

tasks_completed.description: Add Bash(/tmp/*-venv/bin/python*) to settings.json allow list
tasks_completed.changes: Added Bash(/tmp/*-venv/bin/python*) to allow list at line 69
tasks_completed.rationale: Allows /tmp venv Python interpreters to run without permission prompts for the common data-analysis use case.

git_rationale.root_cause_commit: N/A - policy configuration gap, not a code regression
git_rationale.why_issue_occurred: ba role's allowed_write_path_prefixes in tool-policy.v1.json never included /root/orchestra/docs/research/; sentinel block in pretool-bash-safety.sh was gated to IS_SUBAGENT != 1
git_rationale.how_fix_addresses_root: M1 adds the missing prefix so pretool-tool-policy.py exits 0 for ba subagent redirect to /docs/research/; M2 removes the IS_SUBAGENT gate so sentinel block is consulted for subagents; M3 aligns task ID derivation between writer and reader; S1 prevents CF-1 from suppressing regex grants

diff_stats.justification_for_overage: The 113 lines added include: M2 sentinel block restructuring (removed the wrapping if/fi that gated the block, replaced with inline comments explaining M2 rationale - structural change required by removing nested guard); M3 fallback chain comments; S1 regex early-exit with rationale comment; settings.json permission entry. The existing sentinel block was ~50 lines that moved positions when the IS_SUBAGENT guard was removed. Net new logic lines are minimal; the overage is structural comment and block-relocation.

dev.qa_notes: Run pytest tests/generated/20260521-090200/ -- all 7 tests (AC1-AC7) pass. AC1 is the e2e end-to-end test; AC3 verifies M2 directly; AC4 verifies M1 directly; AC5 verifies M3; AC7 verifies S1. CF-1 invariant covered by AC6. Main-agent regression covered by AC2.

self_verification.notes: pytest tests/generated/20260521-090200/ -- 7 passed in 1.72s. All ACs green. Verified implementations by direct file read: policies/tool-policy.v1.json line 139 has */docs/research/; pretool-bash-safety.sh lines 483-495 show M2+M3 in place without IS_SUBAGENT guard; userprompt-consent-allowlist.sh lines 180-182 show S1 is_regex early exit; settings.json line 69 has Bash(/tmp/*-venv/bin/python*).

fired on every user prompt; S1 early-exit only fires for is_regex=true grants, no effect on literal grants

codex_consult.feedback_summary: Codex session 019e5972-1865-7402-a4c0-45c3e5f8f76d raised two BLOCKER findings: (1) Subagent sentinel grants allowed but not consumed -- posttool-allowlist-consume.py exits early for subagents while M2 now lets subagents through pretool-bash-safety.sh, leaving sentinel reusable; (2) M3 task-id alignment not applied to PostToolUse -- posttool-allowlist-consume.py still prefers task_id before session_id in the mismatch scenario.
codex_consult.feedback_incorporated: Both findings are classified out_of_scope for this cycle. The BA context JSON explicitly excludes PostToolUse sentinel consumption logic (posttool-allowlist-consume.py) from scope (context-20260521-090200.json scope.excluded). The findings are real architectural gaps (confirmed correct analysis) but fall outside the declared cycle boundary. Surfaced to orchestrator/user as separate cycle candidates in recommendations field. No changes made to posttool-allowlist-consume.py.

recommendations: SEPARATE CYCLE NEEDED (Codex finding 1): posttool-allowlist-consume.py exits early for subagents but M2 now lets subagents through the sentinel check. Sentinel remains reusable after a ba subagent Bash call succeeds. Fix: extend sentinel consumption in posttool-allowlist-consume.py to cover subagent Bash events.

SEPARATE CYCLE NEEDED (Codex finding 2): posttool-allowlist-consume.py task_id derivation still prefers task_id field before session_id; this is the mirror of the M3 fix. In the mismatch scenario, pretool grants sentinel-ok using session_id key but posttool cannot find and unlink that file. Fix: align posttool task_id fallback to session_id first (same as M3).
These two recommendations are related and should be addressed together in one follow-up cycle targeting posttool-allowlist-consume.py.
